threshold_count: Habitual failure to seek/respond to comfort plus at least 2 social/emotional disturbances
duration_rule: Symptoms present before age 5; child has developmental age of 9+ months

symptoms.description: The patient displays a consistent pattern of inhibited, emotionally withdrawn behavior toward adult caregivers, rarely seeking or responding to comfort when distressed.
The patient exhibits minimal social and emotional responsiveness to others, often accompanied by unprovoked episodes of irritability, sadness, or fearfulness.
The patient exhibits limited positive affect in their interactions with others.
The patient has experienced a pattern of extreme insufficient care, such as social neglect, frequent changes in primary caregivers, or rearing in unusual settings.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least 2 behaviors showing lack of reticence with unfamiliar adults
duration_rule: Child has developmental age of 9+ months

symptoms.description: The patient actively approaches and interacts with unfamiliar adults, lacking the expected reticence or shyness.
The patient exhibits overly familiar verbal or physical behavior with strangers that crosses age-appropriate social boundaries.
The patient fails to check back with an adult caregiver after venturing away, even in unfamiliar settings.
The patient has experienced a pattern of extreme insufficient care, such as social neglect, frequent changes in primary caregivers, or rearing in unusual settings.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 1 intrusion, 1 avoidance, 2 cognitive/mood, and 2 arousal symptoms

symptoms.description: The patient has been directly exposed to, witnessed, or learned about a traumatic event involving actual or threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence.
The patient repeatedly relives the traumatic event through distressing memories, nightmares, or dissociative flashbacks.
The patient actively avoids memories, thoughts, feelings, or external reminders associated with the traumatic event.
The patient is unable to remember an important aspect of the traumatic event, typically due to dissociative amnesia.
The patient holds persistent and exaggerated negative beliefs or expectations about themselves, others, or the world.
The patient experiences persistent, distorted cognitions about the cause or consequences of the traumatic event, leading them to blame themselves or others.
The patient experiences a persistent negative emotional state, such as fear, horror, anger, guilt, or shame.
The patient exhibits markedly diminished interest or participation in significant activities.
The patient experiences feelings of detachment or estrangement from others.
The patient experiences a persistent inability to experience positive emotions, such as an inability to feel happiness, satisfaction, or loving feelings.
The patient exhibits irritable behavior and angry outbursts with little or no provocation, typically expressed as verbal or physical aggression toward people or objects.
The patient engages in reckless or self-destructive behavior.
The patient exhibits hypervigilance.
The patient exhibits an exaggerated startle response.
The patient experiences problems with concentration.
The patient experiences insomnia or other significant sleep disturbances.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 1 intrusion, 1 avoidance OR negative emotion, and 2 physiological symptoms
duration_rule: 1 month or more; child is under age 7

symptoms.description: The patient has been directly exposed to, witnessed, or learned about a traumatic event involving actual or threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence.
The patient repeatedly relives the traumatic event through distressing memories, nightmares, or dissociative flashbacks.
The patient avoids or makes efforts to avoid activities, places, or physical reminders that arouse recollections of the traumatic event.
The patient avoids or makes efforts to avoid people, conversations, or interpersonal situations that arouse recollections of the traumatic event.
The patient experiences a substantially increased frequency of key negative emotional states, such as fear, sadness, guilt, or confusion.
The patient exhibits markedly diminished interest or participation in significant activities, including a constriction of play.
The patient exhibits socially withdrawn behavior.
The patient exhibits a persistent reduction in the expression of positive emotions.
The patient exhibits irritable behavior and angry outbursts with little or no provocation, typically expressed as verbal or physical aggression toward people or objects.
The patient exhibits hypervigilance.
The patient exhibits an exaggerated startle response.
The patient experiences problems with concentration.
The patient experiences insomnia or other significant sleep disturbances.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 9 or more symptoms from any of the five categories (intrusion, mood, dissociation, avoidance, arousal)

symptoms.description: The patient has been directly exposed to, witnessed, or learned about a traumatic event involving actual or threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence.
The patient experiences recurrent, involuntary, and intrusive distressing memories of the traumatic event.
The patient experiences recurrent distressing dreams in which the content or affect of the dream is related to the traumatic event.
The patient experiences dissociative reactions, such as flashbacks, in which they feel or act as if the traumatic event were recurring.

The patient experiences intense or prolonged psychological distress or marked physiological reactions in response to internal or external cues that symbolize or resemble an aspect of the traumatic event.
The patient experiences a persistent inability to experience positive emotions, such as an inability to feel happiness, satisfaction, or loving feelings.
The patient experiences an altered sense of the reality of their surroundings or themselves, such as seeing themselves from another's perspective, being in a daze, or time slowing.
The patient is unable to remember an important aspect of the traumatic event, typically due to dissociative amnesia rather than other factors like head injury or substances.
The patient makes efforts to avoid distressing memories, thoughts, or feelings about or closely associated with the traumatic event.

The patient makes efforts to avoid external reminders, such as people, places, conversations, activities, objects, or situations, that arouse distressing memories, thoughts, or feelings associated with the traumatic event.
The patient experiences insomnia or other significant sleep disturbances.
The patient exhibits irritable behavior and angry outbursts with little or no provocation, typically expressed as verbal or physical aggression toward people or objects.
The patient exhibits hypervigilance.
The patient experiences problems with concentration.
The patient exhibits an exaggerated startle response.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Intense longing/preoccupation plus at least 3 of 8 specified grief symptoms
duration_rule: 12 months (6 months for children); present nearly every day for the past month

symptoms.description: The patient has experienced the death of a close friend or relative.
The patient experiences intense, persistent longing or yearning for the deceased person.
The patient experiences identity disruption, such as feeling as though a part of oneself has died, since the death occurred.
The patient experiences a marked sense of disbelief regarding the death of the deceased.
The patient actively avoids reminders of the deceased person.
The patient experiences intense emotional pain, such as anger, bitter grief, or sorrow, related to the death.
The patient has difficulty moving on with life, such as experiencing problems engaging with friends, pursuing interests, or planning for the future.
The patient experiences emotional numbness, characterized by an absence or marked reduction of emotional reactivity.
The patient feels that life is meaningless or empty without the deceased person.
The patient experiences intense loneliness or feels detached from others following the death.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Distress out of proportion to stressor OR significant functional impairment
duration_rule: Begins within 3 months of stressor; ends within 6 months of stressor termination

symptoms.description: The patient develops emotional or behavioral symptoms in response to an identifiable stressor within 3 months of its onset.
The patient develops emotional or behavioral symptoms in response to a stressor that are out of proportion to the severity or intensity of the stressor.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Other Specified Trauma- and Stressor-Related Disorder
threshold_count: Symptoms characteristic of trauma disorder that cause distress but do not meet full criteria
duration_rule: Variable (e.g., adjustment-like onset > 3 months or duration > 6 months)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences trauma-related symptoms that cause clinically significant distress or impairment but fall below the full diagnostic threshold.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Trauma/stressor symptoms present but insufficient information for specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with trauma-related symptoms causing distress or impairment, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
